reasoning: Magnus Carlsen is currently ranked World #1 by FIDE with a Classical rating of 2837, maintaining a 33-point lead over #2 (Nakamura) on the official June 2025 list. In addition, Carlsen just won the prestigious Norway Chess 2025 tournament in June, while his closest competitors in the live rankings (such as Erigaisi and Nakamura) performed poorly or bled points. Because FIDE publishes ratings monthly, Carlsen's #1 spot on June 29 and July 22 is practically guaranteed (since the rating lists for June and July will already secure him the #1 rank). As for September and December, surpassing a 35+ point rating gap at the absolute elite level requires heavily sustained, dominant performance from competitors and an anomalous sharp decline from Carlsen. Since Carlsen actively played in June 2025, he will not be dropped from the FIDE list due to inactivity in 2025 (which would take 12 months). There remains a small possibility of Carlsen facing an unprecedented classical slump combined with a meteoric surge by Caruana, Gukesh or someone else, so probability naturally decreases slightly for the later dates, but remains overwhelmingly high.
